diagrams.title: Flow Network Example
diagrams.description: A sample flow network with source s, sink t, and intermediate vertices showing capacities on each edge
elements: Directed edges with capacity labels (e.g., s→A:10, A→t:8)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding basic flow network structure and notation

diagrams.title: Residual Graph Construction
diagrams.description: Shows how residual graph is derived from original flow network, with forward and backward edges
elements: Forward edge with residual = capacity - flow
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how residual capacity is calculated and represented

diagrams.title: Augmenting Path Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows an augmenting path highlighted in the residual graph with bottleneck value marked
elements: Path from s to t through intermediate vertices
Minimum capacity edge highlighted as bottleneck
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how flow is increased along augmenting paths

diagrams.title: Max-Flow Min-Cut Theorem Illustration
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the minimum cut that equals maximum flow value
elements: Vertices divided into two sets (S and T)
Cut edges highlighted with their capacities
Source in S side, sink in T side
Cut capacity shown as sum of crossing edges
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof and understanding of max-flow min-cut relationship
